title: Beverly's Secret

jokes.text: You're not goin' off to college, you know. You're spending a week with a family in wisconsin. Jeez, it's just a bit.
jokes.setup: Hank is overpacking for what seems like a major trip
jokes.punchline: Larry deflates it by pointing out it's just a comedy bit for one week
jokes.explanation: Good character work - Larry's exasperation with Hank's grandiosity is perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: You're not goin' off to college, you know. You're spending a week with a family in wisconsin. Jeez, it's just a bit.

jokes.text: Why should Charles kuralt have a monopoly on this kind of shit? / Hank, Charlie's no longer with us. / I know, that's what I'm saying. It's... it's wide open.
jokes.setup: Hank comparing himself to Charles Kuralt's travel reporting
jokes.punchline: Hank sees Kuralt's death as a business opportunity rather than tragedy
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy - Hank's oblivious opportunism is both shocking and perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Why should Charles kuralt have a monopoly on this kind of shit?

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Hank, Charlie's no longer with us.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I know, that's what I'm saying. It's... it's wide open.

jokes.text: I just realized when I said steven wright, you were actually to my right. It's a good thing your name isn't steven left because I'd be looking this way.
jokes.setup: Larry notices the coincidence of Wright's name and position
jokes.punchline: He extends it to the absurd opposite scenario
jokes.explanation: Standard talk show patter - mildly clever but nothing special

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I just realized when I said steven wright, you were actually to my right. It's a good thing your name isn't steven left because I'd be looking this way.

jokes.text: Stephen hawking was mad at me 'cause I took his parking space, and... I said to him, 'don't you have better things to think about?'
jokes.setup: Wright supposedly took Stephen Hawking's parking space
jokes.punchline: His response plays on Hawking's reputation as a brilliant physicist
jokes.explanation: Classic Wright - takes a mundane situation and finds the perfect absurd angle

dialogue.speaker: Steven Wright
dialogue.line: Stephen hawking was mad at me 'cause I took his parking space, and... I said to him, 'don't you have better things to think about?'

jokes.text: I go to lots of the games, uh-huh. I follow the team regularly, yeah. I love going to fenway park... I do not consider myself a fan. I don't want to be labeled.
jokes.setup: Wright describes extensive Red Sox involvement
jokes.punchline: Despite all evidence, refuses the 'fan' label
jokes.explanation: Pure Wright absurdism - the anti-logic is perfectly delivered

dialogue.speaker: Steven Wright
dialogue.line: I go to lots of the games, uh-huh. I follow the team regularly, yeah. I love going to fenway park... I do not consider myself a fan. I don't want to be labeled.

jokes.text: I've been studying Italian for years because I have to study it 'cause I eventually want to teach it.
jokes.setup: Standard question about learning languages
jokes.punchline: Wright's circular non-logic about studying to teach
jokes.explanation: Classic Wright deadpan absurdity with his signature circular reasoning

dialogue.speaker: Steven Wright
dialogue.line: I've been studying Italian for years because I have to study it 'cause I eventually want to teach it.

jokes.text: I've been raising horses for years. They're getting taller and taller, and, um, it's goin' great. You should come over sometime. We'll watch them.
jokes.setup: Question about raising horses
jokes.punchline: Wright takes 'raising' literally - making horses taller, and 'watching' them like TV
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Wright wordplay - multiple meanings twisted into perfect absurdity

dialogue.speaker: Steven Wright
dialogue.line: I've been raising horses for years. They're getting taller and taller, and, um, it's goin' great. You should come over sometime. We'll watch them.

jokes.text: Horses are a fabulously interesting animal. They're one of the only species named that.
jokes.setup: Seems like he'll give interesting horse facts
jokes.punchline: The 'fact' is completely meaningless - they're named 'horses'
jokes.explanation: Perfect anti-joke structure - builds expectation for insight, delivers beautiful nonsense

dialogue.speaker: Steven Wright
dialogue.line: Horses are a fabulously interesting animal. They're one of the only species named that.

jokes.text: I did that once. I lost 15 pounds, but I gained it all back. / I'm not bulimic.
jokes.setup: Mary Lou assumes Beverly is bulimic in the bathroom
jokes.punchline: Beverly's flat denial creates awkward moment
jokes.explanation: Sets up the pregnancy reveal well but not particularly funny on its own

dialogue.speaker: Mary Lou
dialogue.line: I did that once. I lost 15 pounds, but I gained it all back.

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: I'm not bulimic.

jokes.text: I'm pregnant, all right? / Oh, my God. Congratulations. Who's the father? / I don't want to discuss it. / It's not Larry, is it? / Close?
jokes.setup: Beverly reveals pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Mary Lou's immediate assumption it might be Larry
jokes.explanation: Good workplace comedy - the Larry assumption feels natural and cringe-worthy

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: I'm pregnant, all right?

dialogue.speaker: Mary Lou
dialogue.line: Oh, my God. Congratulations. Who's the father?

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: I don't want to discuss it.

dialogue.speaker: Mary Lou
dialogue.line: It's not Larry, is it?

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: Close?

jokes.text: I keep thinking it's Martin lawrence. / Naw, he's too wild. His idea of a first date is driving into a tree.
jokes.setup: Guessing celebrity fathers
jokes.punchline: Reference to Martin Lawrence's erratic behavior/car accidents
jokes.explanation: Topical reference humor - dated but captures the writers' cynical gossiping

dialogue.speaker: Mary Lou
dialogue.line: I keep thinking it's Martin lawrence.

dialogue.speaker: Other Writer
dialogue.line: Naw, he's too wild. His idea of a first date is driving into a tree.

jokes.text: I know things you guys don't want to know about Richard mulligan.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Beverly's wild taste in men
jokes.punchline: Implies shocking info about the deceased character actor
jokes.explanation: Random celebrity name-drop for shock value - feels forced

dialogue.speaker: Writer
dialogue.line: I know things you guys don't want to know about Richard mulligan.

jokes.text: Oh, yeah! Yeah, because he's tired of fucking Claudia schiffer.
jokes.setup: David Copperfield suggested as baby daddy
jokes.punchline: Crude reasoning about his relationship with supermodel
jokes.explanation: Crude shock humor without much craft - relies on vulgarity

dialogue.line: Oh, yeah! Yeah, because he's tired of fucking Claudia schiffer.

jokes.text: I just want to make a straight bet, 50 bucks, Billy dee Williams. / Oh, that is so 1970s.
jokes.setup: Everyone making wild guesses about baby daddy
jokes.punchline: Sid's dated reference gets dismissed
jokes.explanation: Good character beat - Sid being out of touch with current celebrities

dialogue.speaker: Sid
dialogue.line: I just want to make a straight bet, 50 bucks, Billy dee Williams.

dialogue.speaker: Writer
dialogue.line: Oh, that is so 1970s.

jokes.text: I believe the word 'bad' was bandied about.
jokes.setup: Larry asking what's wrong with the sketch
jokes.punchline: Phil's overly formal way of saying Larry called it bad
jokes.explanation: Phil's stuffy delivery makes a simple message funny

dialogue.speaker: Phil
dialogue.line: I believe the word 'bad' was bandied about.

jokes.text: Jimmy caan? / No way. He's still tied up in court.
jokes.setup: Another guess at baby daddy
jokes.punchline: Reference to Caan's legal troubles
jokes.explanation: Lazy topical reference without much craft

dialogue.line: Jimmy caan?
No way. He's still tied up in court.

jokes.text: A lot of people don't know Hank used to work at the house of pies before he worked here.
jokes.setup: Hank making an apple pie on TV
jokes.punchline: Larry's fake biographical detail about House of Pies
jokes.explanation: Larry needling Hank with fake credentials - good character work

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: A lot of people don't know Hank used to work at the house of pies before he worked here.

jokes.text: she was the, uh, first one, uh, in her neighborhood to own a minsk coat. / I think he means 'mink.' / No, she was from minsk.
jokes.setup: Hank talking about his Russian grandmother
jokes.punchline: Confuses her city of origin with fur type
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hank moment - his confident wrongness is endearing and funny

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: she was the, uh, first one, uh, in her neighborhood to own a minsk coat.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I think he means 'mink.'

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: No, she was from minsk.

jokes.text: Hank, I'm no expert on pies, but that one looks like it needs to go back in for another 3 or 4 hours. / You forgot to turn the oven on.
jokes.setup: Hank's pie cooking demonstration
jokes.punchline: The pie is completely raw because he never turned on the oven
jokes.explanation: Classic Hank incompetence - the ultimate cooking show failure

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Hank, I'm no expert on pies, but that one looks like it needs to go back in for another 3 or 4 hours.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: You forgot to turn the oven on.

jokes.text: Why don't you put your head in there, Hank? See if it's working.
jokes.setup: Hank's oven isn't working
jokes.punchline: Larry suggests Hank stick his head in the oven
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that shows Larry's frustration with Hank's incompetence

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Why don't you put your head in there, Hank? See if it's working.

jokes.text: I'm turning into bud melman.
jokes.setup: Larry's frustration with the show's quality
jokes.punchline: References Letterman's hapless character
jokes.explanation: Good industry reference - Larry comparing himself to Letterman's bumbling character

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I'm turning into bud melman.

jokes.text: not a bunch of yokels make Hank look like... an asshole!
jokes.setup: Larry explaining how the bit should work
jokes.punchline: His frustration explodes into calling Hank an asshole
jokes.explanation: Larry's mounting frustration pays off with an outburst

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: not a bunch of yokels make Hank look like... an asshole!

jokes.text: Tonight, didn't it seem more like 54 minutes?
jokes.setup: Larry noting the show is an hour long
jokes.punchline: Suggests it felt shorter due to poor content
jokes.explanation: Self-deprecating meta humor about the show's quality

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Tonight, didn't it seem more like 54 minutes?

jokes.text: lady luck just gave me a blow job.
jokes.setup: Artie wins at poker
jokes.punchline: Crude metaphor for his good fortune
jokes.explanation: Pure Artie - crude but in character for his gambling celebration

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: lady luck just gave me a blow job.

jokes.text: He was drinking again. He was doing so well. 91 days.
jokes.setup: Artie's poker celebration
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's a recovering alcoholic who just relapsed
jokes.explanation: Dark turn that recontextualizes Artie's celebration as tragic

dialogue.line: He was drinking again. He was doing so well. 91 days.

jokes.text: Maybe you should come in a few minutes early each morning, and then you could make some protein shakes, and then, you know, there they'd be.
jokes.setup: Larry wanting his protein shake
jokes.punchline: His completely tone-deaf suggestion that Beverly work more
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry obliviousness - asking pregnant employee to work more hours

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Maybe you should come in a few minutes early each morning, and then you could make some protein shakes, and then, you know, there they'd be.

jokes.text: If you think I'm gonna come in even a minute early, I think you're out of your mind, because I have a life, OK?
jokes.setup: Larry's unreasonable request
jokes.punchline: Beverly's righteous anger explodes
jokes.explanation: Beverly's justified outburst at Larry's cluelessness

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: If you think I'm gonna come in even a minute early, I think you're out of your mind, because I have a life, OK?

jokes.text: What the fuck was that about? / Larry, she's pregnant.
jokes.setup: Larry confused by Beverly's anger
jokes.punchline: Artie's matter-of-fact explanation
jokes.explanation: Artie's blunt delivery contrasts with Larry's confusion

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: What the fuck was that about?

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Larry, she's pregnant.

jokes.text: Well, why didn't, um... she tell me? / Well, we thought she should, but she didn't, 'cause, you know, she didn't want to hurt your feelings. You know how close you two are.
jokes.setup: Larry wondering why Beverly didn't tell him
jokes.punchline: Artie's sarcastic comment about their 'closeness'
jokes.explanation: Artie's dry commentary on Larry's self-centeredness

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Well, why didn't, um... she tell me?

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Well, we thought she should, but she didn't, 'cause, you know, she didn't want to hurt your feelings. You know how close you two are.

jokes.text: You think I should leave E.R?
jokes.setup: La Salle saying he does what he's most afraid of
jokes.punchline: Larry misunderstands and thinks he's considering leaving the show
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - Larry completely misreads the conversation

dialogue.speaker: Eriq La Salle
dialogue.line: You think I should leave E.R?

jokes.text: I said, when I got back, I wanted you gone. / All right. / Did you hear me? / I want him out of here now. He can take his whore with him, OK?
jokes.setup: Hank's remote segment from Wisconsin
jokes.punchline: The host family furiously wants him out
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the Wisconsin bit - Hank has completely destroyed their home

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Schmidt
dialogue.line: I said, when I got back, I wanted you gone.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: All right.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Schmidt
dialogue.line: Did you hear me?

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Schmidt
dialogue.line: I want him out of here now. He can take his whore with him, OK?

jokes.text: Listen to your body. Gimme a porno tape and a pizza. / Ah, comin' right up. / Yes, o best body, I obey.
jokes.setup: Gym trainer says listen to your body
jokes.punchline: Carey's body only wants porn and pizza
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecating humor with nice escalation and commitment to the bit

dialogue.speaker: Drew Carey
dialogue.line: Listen to your body. Gimme a porno tape and a pizza.
Ah, comin' right up.

dialogue.speaker: Drew Carey
dialogue.line: Yes, o best body, I obey.

jokes.text: Chef boyardee, aunt jemima, fightin' Irish. / 'Cause all I can tell you, if you're an American indian, and you're offended by the logo, all I can tell you is, hey, you should have fought harder.
jokes.setup: Carey defending sports logos
jokes.punchline: Suggests Native Americans should have fought harder against colonization
jokes.explanation: Edgy for shock value but not particularly clever - relies on offensive premise

dialogue.speaker: Drew Carey
dialogue.line: Chef boyardee, aunt jemima, fightin' Irish. / 'Cause all I can tell you, if you're an American indian, and you're offended by the logo, all I can tell you is, hey, you should have fought harder.

jokes.text: If women ran the country, there would be no war. / Yeah, I'm sure no one would start a fight for no reason at all if women ran the world.
jokes.setup: Common saying about women and war
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic reference to women starting fights
jokes.explanation: Standard gender humor - not particularly fresh but competently executed

dialogue.line: If women ran the country, there would be no war.
Yeah, I'm sure no one would start a fight for no reason at all if women ran the world.

jokes.text: You mention one hooker, you gotta mention 'em all. You never think they could be so territorial, but Jesus Christ.
jokes.setup: Carey explaining why they couldn't talk about personal life
jokes.punchline: Implies he knows many territorial hookers
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke that builds on the previous setup

dialogue.speaker: Drew Carey
dialogue.line: You mention one hooker, you gotta mention 'em all. You never think they could be so territorial, but Jesus Christ.

jokes.text: Great to be here at caesars. I've only been here a day. I'm already up 10 grand. Of course, I had to let a fat arab fuck me up the ass, but, hey.
jokes.setup: Carey testing new Vegas material
jokes.punchline: Extremely crude prostitution joke
jokes.explanation: Crude shock humor with offensive stereotypes - lazy writing relying on vulgarity

dialogue.speaker: Drew Carey
dialogue.line: Great to be here at caesars. I've only been here a day. I'm already up 10 grand. Of course, I had to let a fat arab fuck me up the ass, but, hey.

jokes.text: I think women are like snow flakes, you know, you have to have... / are you fucking my secretary?
jokes.setup: Carey starting a romantic metaphor
jokes.punchline: Larry's blunt interruption
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry moment - cuts through BS to ask what he really wants to know

dialogue.speaker: Drew Carey
dialogue.line: I think women are like snow flakes, you know, you have to have...

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: are you fucking my secretary?

jokes.text: are you fucking my secretary? No, you want me to?
jokes.setup: Larry asks directly about Beverly
jokes.punchline: Carey's flippant response
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation with great economy. Carey's response perfectly deflects Larry's directness.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: are you fucking my secretary?

dialogue.speaker: Drew Carey
dialogue.line: No, you want me to?

jokes.text: basically messed up and got his girlfriend pregnant, and they had a baby. / Talk about human, right?
jokes.setup: La Salle describing his character's storyline
jokes.punchline: Larry's pointed comment about how 'human' that story is
jokes.explanation: Larry making the obvious parallel to Beverly's situation

dialogue.speaker: Eriq La Salle
dialogue.line: basically messed up and got his girlfriend pregnant, and they had a baby.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Talk about human, right?

jokes.text: I'm pregnant. / Hey, the more, the merrier.
jokes.setup: Beverly tells La Salle she's pregnant
jokes.punchline: His inappropriately casual response
jokes.explanation: Good awkward beat - completely wrong response to pregnancy news

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: I'm pregnant.

dialogue.speaker: Eriq La Salle
dialogue.line: Hey, the more, the merrier.

jokes.text: They're just a pair of lungs with a dick.
jokes.setup: Discussion about singers and sex
jokes.punchline: Reductive description of male singers
jokes.explanation: Crude but memorable description - captures writers' cynical view

dialogue.line: They're just a pair of lungs with a dick.

jokes.text: Yes! Thank you! / You... whoa, whoa, whoa!
jokes.setup: Beverly telling La Salle about pregnancy
jokes.punchline: His enthusiastic reaction followed by immediate backpedaling
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy beat - his excitement immediately turns to panic

dialogue.speaker: Eriq La Salle
dialogue.line: Yes! Thank you!

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: You... whoa, whoa, whoa!

jokes.text: Still get the phantom tangle.
jokes.setup: Larry commenting on Bolton's new short haircut
jokes.punchline: Bolton's reference to phantom pain from his former long hair
jokes.explanation: Clever reference to phantom limb syndrome applied to hair - good self-aware humor

dialogue.speaker: Michael Bolton
dialogue.line: Still get the phantom tangle.

jokes.text: First of all, I did not fuck your assistant. / She's a fan of mine, she's been to a lot of shows, and I don't fuck all my fans, OK?
jokes.setup: Larry accusing Bolton of sleeping with Beverly
jokes.punchline: Bolton's defense implies he does sleep with some fans
jokes.explanation: Good inadvertent admission - his denial actually makes him look worse

dialogue.speaker: Michael Bolton
dialogue.line: First of all, I did not fuck your assistant. / She's a fan of mine, she's been to a lot of shows, and I don't fuck all my fans, OK?

jokes.text: That haircut is gay. Do you know that? / Seriously? / My haircut's gay? / No. Their haircut.
jokes.setup: Larry insulting Bolton's haircut
jokes.punchline: Confusing exchange about whose haircut is being discussed
jokes.explanation: Weak payoff - the pronoun confusion doesn't add much to the insult

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: That haircut is gay. Do you know that?

dialogue.speaker: Michael Bolton
dialogue.line: Seriously?

dialogue.speaker: Michael Bolton
dialogue.line: My haircut's gay?

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: No. Their haircut.

jokes.text: I think I put that guy in his place. / Who, Michael? / Yeah. / Well, I'm glad you feel good about it, because it turns out that it's la salle.
jokes.setup: Larry feeling good about confronting Bolton
jokes.punchline: Artie reveals Bolton wasn't even the father
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedy of errors payoff - Larry attacked the wrong guy after all that investigation

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I think I put that guy in his place.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Who, Michael?

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Yeah.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Well, I'm glad you feel good about it, because it turns out that it's la salle.

jokes.text: We should send bolton some flowers.
jokes.setup: Larry just verbally attacked an innocent Bolton
jokes.punchline: Artie's dry suggestion they apologize with flowers
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artie understatement - treating the disaster like a minor social gaffe

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: We should send bolton some flowers.

tmdb_overview: Hank does a remote from Wisconsin. The staff tries to guess the father of Beverly's baby.
